title: Checkpoints and /rollback
summary: Explains Hermes's opt-in checkpoint system that automatically snapshots project files before destructive operations, with /rollback for restore, single-file restore, and storage management.

cli_commands.purpose: Show checkpoint store size and per-project breakdown

config_keys.description: Hard cap on total store size in MB
Skip individual files larger than this in MB
Days before orphan/stale checkpoints are pruned
Delete checkpoint entries whose working directory no longer exists

slash_commands.purpose: List all checkpoints with change stats
Restore to checkpoint N (also undoes last chat turn)
Preview diff between checkpoint N and current state
Restore a single file from checkpoint N

code_examples.caption: Enable checkpoints
code_examples.code:
hermes chat --checkpoints
# Or globally in config.yaml:
# checkpoints:
#   enabled: true

code_examples.caption: Full checkpoint configuration
code_examples.code:
checkpoints:
  enabled: false
  max_snapshots: 20
  max_total_size_mb: 500
  max_file_size_mb: 10
  auto_prune: true
  retention_days: 7
  delete_orphans: true
  min_interval_hours: 24

code_examples.caption: Inspect and manage the store
code_examples.code:
hermes checkpoints
hermes checkpoints prune --retention-days 3 --max-size-mb 200
hermes checkpoints clear-legacy

gotchas: Checkpoints are opt-in — enabled: false is the default; storage grows if not managed
At most one checkpoint per directory per turn is created — not one per tool call
Directories with more than 50,000 files are skipped (e.g., node_modules heavy repos)
Files larger than max_file_size_mb (default 10 MB) are excluded from snapshots
If git is not in PATH, checkpoints are transparently disabled
/rollback N also undoes the last conversation turn — the agent's context matches the filesystem
Legacy v1 archives are not automatically pruned unless retention_days elapses; use clear-legacy manually
full_text:
# Checkpoints and /rollback

# Checkpoints and `/rollback`

Hermes Agent can automatically snapshot your project before **destructive operations** and restore it with a single command. Checkpoints are **opt-in** as of v2 — the default is off.

Enable checkpoints per-session with `--checkpoints`:

```bash
hermes chat --checkpoints
```

Or enable globally in `~/.hermes/config.yaml`:

```yaml
checkpoints:
  enabled: true
```

This safety net is powered by an internal **Checkpoint Manager** that keeps a single shared shadow git repository under `~/.hermes/checkpoints/store/` — your real project `.git` is never touched.

## What Triggers a Checkpoint

Checkpoints are taken automatically before:

- **File tools** — `write_file` and `patch`
- **Destructive terminal commands** — `rm`, `rmdir`, `cp`, `install`, `mv`, `sed -i`, `truncate`, `dd`, `shred`, output redirects (`>`), and `git reset`/`clean`/`checkout`

The agent creates **at most one checkpoint per directory per turn**.

## Quick Reference

In-session slash commands:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/rollback` | List all checkpoints with change stats |
| `/rollback <N>` | Restore to checkpoint N (also undoes last chat turn) |
| `/rollback diff <N>` | Preview diff between checkpoint N and current state |
| `/rollback <N> <file>` | Restore a single file from checkpoint N |

CLI for inspecting and managing the store:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `hermes checkpoints` | Show total size, project count, per-project breakdown |
| `hermes checkpoints status` | Same as bare `checkpoints` |
| `hermes checkpoints prune` | Force a sweep: delete orphans/stale, GC, enforce size cap |
| `hermes checkpoints clear` | Nuke the entire checkpoint base (asks first) |
| `hermes checkpoints clear-legacy` | Delete only `legacy-*` archives from v1 migration |

## How Checkpoints Work

1. Hermes detects when tools are about to **modify files** in your working tree
2. Once per conversation turn (per directory), it:
   - Resolves a reasonable project root for the file
   - Initialises or reuses the **single shared shadow store** at `~/.hermes/checkpoints/store/`
   - Stages into a per-project index, builds a tree, commits to a per-project ref (`refs/hermes/<project-hash>`)
3. Per-project refs form a checkpoint history you can inspect and restore via `/rollback`

## Configuration

```yaml
checkpoints:
  enabled: false              # master switch (default: false — opt-in)
  max_snapshots: 20           # max checkpoints per project
  max_total_size_mb: 500      # hard cap on total store size
  max_file_size_mb: 10        # skip files larger than this

  # Auto-maintenance
  auto_prune: true
  retention_days: 7
  delete_orphans: true
  min_interval_hours: 24
```

## Listing Checkpoints

```
/rollback
```

Example output:

```text
📸 Checkpoints for /path/to/project:

  1. 4270a8c  2026-03-16 04:36  before patch  (1 file, +1/-0)
  2. eaf4c1f  2026-03-16 04:35  before write_file
  3. b3f9d2e  2026-03-16 04:34  before terminal: sed -i s/old/new/ config.py  (1 file, +1/-1)
```

## Inspecting the Store

```bash
hermes checkpoints
```

Shows total size, projects, per-project breakdown, and legacy archives.

Force a full sweep:
```bash
hermes checkpoints prune --retention-days 3 --max-size-mb 200
```

## Restoring with `/rollback`

```
/rollback 1
```

Hermes:
1. Verifies the target commit exists in the shadow store
2. Takes a **pre-rollback snapshot** so you can "undo the undo"
3. Restores tracked files in your working directory
4. **Undoes the last conversation turn** so the agent's context matches

## Single-File Restore

```
/rollback 1 src/broken_file.py
```

## Safety and Performance Guards

- **Git availability** — if `git` is not found on `PATH`, checkpoints are transparently disabled
- **Directory scope** — skips root `/`, home `$HOME`
- **Repository size** — directories with more than 50,000 files are skipped
- **Per-file size cap** — files larger than `max_file_size_mb` (default 10 MB) excluded
- **Total store size cap** — when store exceeds `max_total_size_mb`, oldest commits dropped round-robin
- **No-change snapshots** — if no changes since last snapshot, checkpoint is skipped
- **Non-fatal errors** — all errors logged at debug level; tools continue to run

## Where Checkpoints Live

```text
~/.hermes/checkpoints/
  ├── store/                 # single shared bare git repo
  │   ├── HEAD, objects/     # git internals (shared across projects)
  │   ├── refs/hermes/<hash> # per-project branch tip
  │   ├── indexes/<hash>     # per-project git index
  │   ├── projects/<hash>.json  # workdir + timestamps
  │   └── info/exclude
  ├── .last_prune            # auto-prune idempotency marker
  └── legacy-<ts>/           # archived pre-v2 per-project shadow repos
```

### Migration from v1

Pre-v2 shadow repos moved into `~/.hermes/checkpoints/legacy-<timestamp>/` on first v2 run. Run:

```bash
hermes checkpoints clear-legacy
```

to reclaim space after verifying you don't need the old history.

## Best Practices

- **Enable checkpoints only when you need them** — `hermes chat --checkpoints`
- **Use `/rollback diff` before restoring** — preview what will change
- **Use `/rollback` instead of `git reset`** for agent-driven changes
- **Check `hermes checkpoints status` occasionally** if using regularly
- **Combine with Git worktrees** for maximum safety
